stories.headline: US and Iran exchange strikes and accuse each other of violating ceasefire

stories.headline: Three firefighters die battling huge wildfires on Colorado-Utah border
stories.summary: A number of blazes have been raging across Utah and Colorado, leading both governors to declare states of emergency.

stories.headline: Three Firefighters Die as Wildfires Ravage Utah and Colorado
stories.summary: Their bodies were brought by helicopter to a Colorado airport, where their soot-covered comrades drove them to the coroner’s office. Fires are raging in the arid Southwest after a warm winter.

stories.summary: While the Iran ceasefire remains intact despite recent exchanges of fire, Former Deputy National Security Advisor Ben Rhodes warned it could unravel if tit-for-tat strikes escalate or hardliners on either side gain the upper hand. stories.headline: At Least 4 Dead in Kentucky Flooding, Governor Says
stories.summary: Gov. Andy Beshear of Kentucky said emergency crews rescued people from flooded homes and vehicles. Flash flood warnings continued on Sunday.

stories.headline: Why the US Needs its Neighbors for Energy
stories.summary: Unlike autos or agriculture, North America’s energy trade does not function as a single, integrated three-country system. Instead, it operates through two powerful bilateral relationships: Canadian oil flowing south into US refineries and American natural gas powering Mexico’s economy. stories.headline: Burnham’s Rise Revives Talk of War Bonds to Fund the UK Military
stories.summary: Andy Burnham, Britain’s soon-to-be prime minister, wants an array of bold new policies to attract voters who have grown tired of a Labour government mired in indecision and political backbiting. One idea is the issuing of war bonds.

stories.headline: Julia Letlow, Lifted by Trump, Wins Republican Senate Runoff in Louisiana
stories.summary: If she reaches the Senate, Ms. Letlow would replace Senator Bill Cassidy and become the first G.O.P. woman to represent Louisiana in the chamber.

stories.headline: Venezuela earthquakes: death toll rises again to more than 1,400

stories.headline: Protesters in Serbia doubt Vucic resignation will bring change

stories.headline: Second World War 'hell ship' found in Philippines with over 1,000 prisoners
stories.summary: Divers have found the wreck of the Hofuku Maru off Luzon in the Philippines. The Japanese freighter sank in 1944 with over 1,200 Allied POWs; a digitised military file finally solved the mystery.

stories.headline: Private Credit Quietly Backs the Craze Propping Up US Consumers
stories.summary: Private credit companies are increasingly backing the "buy now, pay later" craze that's propping up US consumers. This trend is throwing up red flags for credit raters, former regulatory chiefs and others on guard for potential risks. René Ismail explains  (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: Venezuela’s Machado Attempts Fast Return Amid Quake Response
stories.summary: Venezuela’s opposition leader María Corina Machado is working to return to the country as soon as possible, according to people with direct knowledge of her plans, a move that could reshape the political landscape as acting President Delcy Rodríguez struggles to respond to the nation’s worst natural disaster in decades.

stories.headline: Aramco Helicopter Crash in Ras Tanura Kills All 14 on Board
stories.summary: A helicopter operated by Saudi Aramco crashed Sunday in the energy heartland of Ras Tanura, killing all 14 passengers on board.

stories.headline: Can USMCA Survive Trump’s Second Term?
stories.summary: As Canada, Mexico, and the United States begin reviewing the USMCA trade pact, former diplomats and trade negotiators say the stakes extend far beyond tariffs. Former Canadian Ambassador Kirsten Hillman, former Mexican Ambassador Gerónimo Gutiérrez, and former US trade counsel Kelly Ann Shaw argue that the agreement now underpins everything from supply chains and agriculture to intellectual property and investment. stories.headline: Leverage That Fueled US Stock Rally Becomes a Growing Concern
stories.summary: The leverage that helped fuel the US stock rally is now becoming an increasing source of unease.

stories.headline: Europe’s Hunt for AI Stocks Leads to Power Suppliers and Banks
stories.summary: Investors who want additional exposure to artificial intelligence in Europe are getting creative, searching out companies that enable the technology or stand to benefit from it as the usual suspects get more pricey.

stories.headline: Sydney Home Auction Activity Weakens as Costs, Tax Changes Weigh
stories.summary: Sydney posted its weakest weekend home auction clearance rate in more than six years as higher interest rates and property tax changes dampen activity and weigh on prices, after years of rapid gains.

stories.headline: Colorado’s Primary for Governor Pivots to Trump and Who Will Fight Him
